tldr: Anna van der Breggen crushed the 12.7 km uphill time trial to Nevegal, winning in 31:38 — 1:04 clear of TT world champion Marlen Reusser and 1:10 over Demi Vollering — to take the maglia rosa off Elisa Balsamo and seize control of the GC.

narrative.headline: Van der Breggen demolishes the field in the Nevegal TT
narrative.body:
The first true GC day was a 12.7 km uphill time trial from Belluno to Nevegal, and Anna van der Breggen turned it into a statement. She was already 36 seconds up on Vollering at the first intermediate split and only extended her advantage on the upper slopes, stopping the clock at 31:38.

Marlen Reusser, the TT world champion, rode the discipline she dominates but could not match the climber — even after starting on a road bike and accelerating as the gradients steepened, she finished 1:04 down. Vollering went out fast, quicker than Reusser at the first checkpoint, but faded on the climb to come home 1:10 back in third.

The result transformed the race. After three days of Balsamo sprints with the GC bunched, van der Breggen took the maglia rosa with a real cushion over every rival, and the time-trial gaps would frame the GC battle all the way to the final weekend.

gcImpact.headline: Van der Breggen takes pink with a commanding lead
gcImpact.body: Because it was an individual time trial, the stage result equals the GC: van der Breggen in pink, Reusser 2nd at +1:04, Vollering 3rd at +1:10, Niedermaier 4th at +1:26, Trinca Colonel 5th at +1:31, Dickson +1:38, de Vries +1:39, Longo Borghini +1:51. Balsamo, the previous leader, dropped out of GC contention on the climb.

riderPerformances.name: Anna van der Breggen
riderPerformances.narrative: A dominant uphill TT, winning by over a minute and taking the maglia rosa. The ride established her as the GC leader and the rider everyone else had to overturn on the mountains — a position she held until the final day.

keyMoments.what: Already 36s up on Vollering at the first intermediate split

riderPerformances.name: Marlen Reusser
riderPerformances.narrative: The TT world champion was beaten by 1:04 on a course too steep for her, but 2nd place moved her into 2nd overall — her high point of the race before the high mountains.

riderPerformances.name: Demi Vollering
riderPerformances.narrative: Started faster than Reusser but faded on the upper slopes, conceding 1:10 to van der Breggen — a deficit she would chase for the rest of the race and ultimately overturn on the final day.

riderPerformances.name: Antonia Niedermaier
riderPerformances.narrative: Fourth on the day, limiting her losses to keep herself in the GC mix.

riderPerformances.name: Elisa Longo Borghini
riderPerformances.narrative: Lost significant time to her GC rivals in the TT, an 8th place that left her with ground to make up in the mountains.

incidents.notes: Shirin van Anrooij (Lidl - Trek) did not start the stage due to illness.

sources.title: Stage 4: Van der Breggen crushes Vollering and Reusser in Nevegal TT to take pink
Van der Breggen crushes opposition with stunning uphill TT on Stage 4
